subheadline: Zergboard gives you a Trello-simple interface with Linear-level structure, multi-tenant workspaces, and an API-first core so your agent flows can create, update, and resolve work automatically.
value_proposition: A lightweight kanban board with multi-tenant workspaces and an API-first architecture designed for teams and AI agent automation.
target_audience: Small-to-mid-size product teams and developers building agent-driven or automated workflows who want Trello's simplicity with Linear's structure.

pricing.price_anchoring: Free tier anchors entry, $12/seat Core positioned as Trello replacement, $39 Growth for multi-project teams, $149 Scale for enterprise-grade needs; annual billing discount (~15%) mentioned to nudge commitment

features: Kanban cards and columns with drag-and-drop
API-first architecture for automation, bots, and agents
Superuser admin panel for platform-level governance
differentiators: Multi-tenant by default — not bolted on
API-first core built explicitly for agent and automation flows
Deploy-ready on Fly.io with portable stack (Nuxt + Postgres)
Positioned as the middle ground between Trello bloat and Linear complexity

design_patterns.layout: Single-column scroll with hero, live demo snapshot, feature grid, pricing table, and FAQ accordion
design_patterns.color_scheme: Not determinable from text alone, but technical/SaaS conventions implied
design_patterns.typography: Clean, concise — short punchy headers with utilitarian body copy
notable_patterns: Live work snapshot with real-time card counts as social proof substitute
Agent flow update callout (7 tickets from QA failures) as dynamic demo moment

key_objections_addressed: Can AI agents use this? — Yes, API is minimal and stable for automation
Does it support multiple clients/tenants? — Yes, isolated orgs with membership access control
Can I self-host? — Yes, Nuxt + Postgres with portable deploy primitives
Is there a free tier? — Yes, up to 3 users
missing_or_weak: Zero social proof — no customer logos, testimonials, or user counts
No press mentions or third-party validation
No specifics on 'Built for teams and agents' — agent ecosystem examples are vague
No screenshots or product demo beyond a text-based card count widget
No comparison table vs Trello or Linear despite explicitly invoking both
No case studies or use-case stories
Trust signals entirely absent — no security certs, uptime SLA, data residency info
'Talk to sales' CTA for Scale tier with no sales contact detail or calendar link visible
Superuser admin feature listed without explanation of what governance means in practice
standout_elements: Sharp, memorable positioning: 'Trello-simple, Linear-level structure' in one sentence
Agent-first messaging is differentiated and timely — most PM tools treat automation as an afterthought
FAQ section directly neutralizes four common purchase blockers
Pricing is transparent, self-serve, and cleanly tiered without feature-gating confusion
Self-hosting path explicitly called out — appeals to technical buyers
Live snapshot with 'Agent flow updated 7 tickets from QA failures' is a creative, concrete demo moment
